content:
First note that if \(n<17\) then
\[
n^2-15n-27<0,
\]
while the product of the digits of \(n\) is \(\ge0\), so no solutions there.  Hence \(n\ge17\).

-- For a two‐digit \(n\), say \(n=10a+b\), the digit‐product is \(ab\le9\cdot9=81\).  
Solve
\[
n^2-15n-27\le81
\;\Longrightarrow\;
n^2-15n-108\le0,
\]
whose positive root is about \(20.3\).  Thus we only need to check \(17,18,19,20\):

17: digits \(1,7\) ⇒ product \(7\),  
  \(17^2-15\cdot17-27=289-255-27=7\).  OK.

18: product \(8\),  
  \(18^2-15\cdot18-27=324-270-27=27\neq8\).

19: product \(9\),  
  \(19^2-15\cdot19-27=361-285-27=49\neq9\).

20: product \(0\),  
  \(20^2-15\cdot20-27=400-300-27=73\neq0\).

No other two‐digit \(n\ge17\) can work because \(n^2-15n-27>81\) for \(n\ge21\).

-- For three or more digits, one checks that
\[
n^2-15n-27
\]
exceeds the maximal possible digit‐product \(9^d\) once \(n\) is large enough—and in fact already for \(n\ge37\) the quadratic
exceeds \(9^3=729\).  No new solutions occur.

Hence the only solution is \(n=17\), and the sum of all solutions is
\[
\boxed{17}.
\]

message.content:
First note that if \(n<17\) then
\[
n^2-15n-27<0,
\]
while the product of the digits of \(n\) is \(\ge0\), so no solutions there.  Hence \(n\ge17\).

-- For a two‐digit \(n\), say \(n=10a+b\), the digit‐product is \(ab\le9\cdot9=81\).  
Solve
\[
n^2-15n-27\le81
\;\Longrightarrow\;
n^2-15n-108\le0,
\]
whose positive root is about \(20.3\).  Thus we only need to check \(17,18,19,20\):

17: digits \(1,7\) ⇒ product \(7\),  
  \(17^2-15\cdot17-27=289-255-27=7\).  OK.

18: product \(8\),  
  \(18^2-15\cdot18-27=324-270-27=27\neq8\).

19: product \(9\),  
  \(19^2-15\cdot19-27=361-285-27=49\neq9\).

20: product \(0\),  
  \(20^2-15\cdot20-27=400-300-27=73\neq0\).

No other two‐digit \(n\ge17\) can work because \(n^2-15n-27>81\) for \(n\ge21\).

-- For three or more digits, one checks that
\[
n^2-15n-27
\]
exceeds the maximal possible digit‐product \(9^d\) once \(n\) is large enough—and in fact already for \(n\ge37\) the quadratic
exceeds \(9^3=729\).  No new solutions occur.

Hence the only solution is \(n=17\), and the sum of all solutions is
\[
\boxed{17}.
\]
